task_completion.description: Create a hierarchical diagram with OLT, Primary Splitter, Secondary Splitters, and 3 Customer Homes/ONTs
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tree-like diagram in draw.io starting from an OLT node, connecting to a primary splitter (1:4), which connects to secondary splitters (1:8), and finally connecting to 3 distinct House or ONT nodes.

task_completion.description: Label the fiber segments with distances
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels on or adjacent to the lines (fiber segments) connecting the shapes, indicating distance values (e.g., in km).

task_completion.description: Include calculated received signal strength for House A
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text label next to House A (or ONT A) showing a dBm value. The value should be approximately -19.67 dBm (accept within ±0.5 dBm).

task_completion.description: Include calculated received signal strength for House B
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text label next to House B (or ONT B) showing a dBm value. The value should be approximately -20.51 dBm (accept within ±0.5 dBm).

task_completion.description: Include calculated received signal strength for House C
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text label next to House C (or ONT C) showing a dBm value. The value should be approximately -19.09 dBm (accept within ±0.5 dBm).

task_completion.description: Save the diagram as ftth_design.drawio and export as ftth_design.pdf
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io window title shows 'ftth_design.drawio'. Additionally, look for an export dialog, a success message for PDF export, or 'ftth_design.pdf' visible on the Desktop or in a file manager.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io (diagrams.net) application interface with shapes being dragged, dropped, and connected, rather than a text editor modifying XML directly.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered the calculated dBm values into the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: The dBm values should be visible as text elements within the draw.io canvas, indicating they were typed into the diagram.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct calculated received signal strengths are: House A = -19.67 dBm, House B = -20.51 dBm, and House C = -19.09 dBm. Accept values within ±0.5 dBm of these targets. The diagram must contain at least 8 shapes and include the keywords 'olt', 'splitter', and 'ont' (or 'house').
